essence: David lays out the agentic OS vision (vertical stack: file-system second brain -> Claude harness -> chat/voice/Telegram/OMI; horizontal stack: 5-Mac Tailscale mesh across Thailand/Philippines), commits to same-day Lars deliverables (Loom week-in-review, JSON->NotebookLM agentic OS presentation) after the M4 Pro screen died, and sets Joy three shop KPIs (daily revenue visibility, daily social posts, daily AI conversation) toward 50k THB/month.

actions.text: Write a strong JSON description of the agentic OS vertical/horizontal stacks and import into NotebookLM to generate the Lars presentation
Record a Loom week-in-review for Lars covering the week's work plus agentic and personal stacks
Get Ecamm Live and the Blackmagic 4K camera working on the M4 main so it becomes the primary machine today
Design an inbox/router layer for the to-do system that leans on the second-brain index topology
Jan to produce a Nano Banana 2 infographic 8-9 video series for AI-TLDR mirroring the agentic OS build

essence: David sketches a lightweight to-do/journal micro-app for the todo brain — a list with minimal fields, time-based daily grouping, and roll-forward/drop mechanics at file boundaries — deciding to model it as an ongoing journal rather than a historical log.
David questions whether warranty repair of his failed MacBook Pro really requires posting it to Bangkok and back, surprised Chiang Mai (Thailand's second city) may have no Apple repair centre.

David drafts a Facebook-group post asking the Chiang Mai community where the official Apple warranty service centre is for his 10-month-old Australian-bought MacBook Pro with a dead screen, noting Studio 7 shops but unclear authorisation.

actions.text: Clean up and post the warranty-repair help request to the Chiang Mai Facebook group

essence: David logs the MacBook Pro 14-inch display failure pattern — interlacing plus a horizontal black line, then full blackout with external monitors still working and a brief 5-minute recovery — concluding it is a hardware display fault, not software.

With the M4 Pro screen dead, David plans an emergency migration making the M4 Mini his primary machine (replicating browser/terminal environment, FliDeck working) and a video update email to Lars, with the repair needing resolution within two weeks before a Vietnam conference requiring a mobile platform.

actions.text: Send video update email to Lars
Replicate MacBook Pro environment and get FliDeck video conversation working on the M4 Mini
Resolve MacBook Pro screen repair within two weeks before Vietnam conference travel

essence: David confirms the numbered project subfolders each live in their own individual GitHub repo rather than all pushing to a single monorepo.
David asks how to add an SSH shell alias targeting the MacBook Pro to match his existing m2/m4 machine aliases for cross-machine access.

David gripes that Claude Code keeps re-asking folder-trust for known folders and asks how to run the repo refresh tool across all repos without creating a duplicate JSON report when one already exists.
David wants a script that commits and pushes all the 00x-numbered subfolders (000, 005, 006, 007, Archive) in one pass, first checking each folder has a git remote configured.

actions.text: Build a script to commit and push all 00x subfolders with remote-existence checking
